methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rhoman Aerospace is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The comparable universe consists of 7 private companies, including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air, with no public market comps available for direct reference.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. While 5 same-sector peers exist within Drones & Autonomous, the dataset contains zero companies meeting same-stage filters, zero at adjacent stages, and zero within similar valuation tiers. The minimum threshold of three datapoints necessary for reliable peer-median triangulation could not be satisfied. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise and reduce analytical integrity.

Without a sufficiently dense and stage-appropriate comparable set, traditional multiples-based approaches cannot be responsibly deployed. Valuation of Rhoman Aerospace requires alternative methods—such as DCF analysis of projected cash flows, venture capital method modeling using target exit values and required returns, or qualitative assessment of technology differentiation and market timing relative to the named peer set. The primary constraint is the sparsity of seed-stage reference points in this nascent sector. Key uncertainties include technology maturity relative to better-capitalized peers, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital requirements to reach meaningful revenue inflection.
